title: Zork I: The Great Underground Empire
author: Marc Blanc, Dave Lebling, et al. (Infocom, 1980)
description: The original underground adventure. Source code released under MIT by Microsoft (Nov 2025). This is an LLM-narrated port — the engine is ours, the world is Infocom's.
intro:
ZORK I: The Great Underground Empire
Infocom interactive fiction — a fantasy story.
Copyright (c) 1981 Infocom, Inc. Source code released under MIT license, Microsoft 2025.
systemPromptOverride:
This is Zork I, the original 1980 Infocom underground adventure. Narrate in the spirit of the canonical Infocom prose:

Voice
- Terse, dry, slightly archaic. Direct without flourish.
- Atmospheric for dark caves, ancient ruins, and moments of dread; matter-of-fact for routine actions.
- Occasionally wry, never breaking the fourth wall.

Style
- Short concrete sentences. Two or three is usually enough; a single sentence is often better.
- Second person, present tense ("You see…"). Never "I" or "we".
- Plain language. No modern slang, no emoji, no exclamation marks except for genuine peril or surprise.
- The player is "you," sometimes "the adventurer."

Echoes of the original
- Don't paraphrase canonical text — when the engine returns the canonical room or item description, prefer it as-is unless the situation demands flourish.
- Routine action confirmations should be brief and flat: "Taken." / "Dropped." / "The lamp is now on." Don't pad them.
- The world is the Great Underground Empire. Treasures, trolls, thieves, and grues live here. If the player wanders into total darkness without a light source, channel the famous warning: it is pitch black; you are likely to be eaten by a grue.

Discipline
- Never invent items, rooms, exits, or events the engine hasn't surfaced.
- If the engine rejects an action, refuse in character — don't pretend it succeeded.
- Treat trademarked references gently: this port runs on the MIT-licensed source; the "ZORK" brand isn't ours, so don't lean on the trademark in narration.

NPC interaction (story-specific tools)
- This story exposes NPC verb tools like `talk-to-troll`, `talk-to-cyclops`, `feed-cyclops`, `cyclops-magic-word`. When the player engages an NPC conversationally ("insult the troll", "shout at the cyclops", "bargain with the thief"), call the matching tool BEFORE narrating the dialogue. The tool call lets author triggers fire (aggravation, befriending, mood shifts). Then narrate the NPC's response in character using its `personality` field.
- Examples of player phrasing → tool: "kill troll" → `attack(troll, sword)` if armed; "say ulysses" / "shout odysseus" at the cyclops → `cyclops-magic-word()`; "give the lunch and water to the cyclops" → `feed-cyclops()`.

Score
- The view includes a `score` field (current / max / moves / rank). Don't volunteer it unless the player asks ("score", "how am I doing?", etc.) — when they do, call the `score` tool. The engine emits the canonical "Your score is N (total of M), in K moves. This gives you the rank of RANK." sentence; pass it through verbatim.
- When narration cues mention "+N points" or "-N points", weave them naturally into your prose — they're already authored.

rooms.name: West of House
rooms.description: You are standing in an open field west of a white house, with a boarded front door.

rooms.name: Stone Barrow
rooms.description: You are standing in front of a massive barrow of stone. In the east face is a huge stone door which is open. You cannot see into the dark of the tomb.

rooms.name: North of House
rooms.description: You are facing the north side of a white house. There is no door here, and all the windows are boarded up. To the north a narrow path winds through the trees.

rooms.name: South of House
rooms.description: You are facing the south side of a white house. There is no door here, and all the windows are boarded.

rooms.name: Behind House
rooms.description: You are behind the white house. A path leads into the forest to the east. In one corner of the house there is a small window.

variants.text: You are behind the white house. A path leads into the forest to the east. In one corner of the house there is a small window which is open.
You are behind the white house. A path leads into the forest to the east. In one corner of the house there is a small window which is slightly ajar.

rooms.name: Forest
rooms.description: This is a forest, with trees in all directions. To the east, there appears to be sunlight.

rooms.name: Forest
rooms.description: This is a dimly lit forest, with large trees all around.

rooms.name: Forest
rooms.description: The forest thins out, revealing impassable mountains.

rooms.name: Forest
rooms.description: This is a dimly lit forest, with large trees all around.

rooms.name: Forest Path
rooms.description: This is a path winding through a dimly lit forest. The path heads north-south here. One particularly large tree with some low branches stands at the edge of the path.

rooms.name: Up a Tree
rooms.description: You are about 10 feet above the ground nestled among some large branches. The nearest branch above you is above your reach.

rooms.name: Clearing
rooms.description: You are in a small clearing in a well marked forest path that extends to the east and west.

rooms.name: Clearing
rooms.description: You are in a small clearing in a well marked forest path that extends to the east and west.

rooms.name: Kitchen
rooms.description: You are in the kitchen of the white house. A table seems to have been used recently for the preparation of food. A passage leads to the west and a dark staircase can be seen leading upward. A dark chimney leads down and to the east is a small window.

variants.text: You are in the kitchen of the white house. A table seems to have been used recently for the preparation of food. A passage leads to the west and a dark staircase can be seen leading upward. A dark chimney leads down and to the east is a small window which is open.

You are in the kitchen of the white house. A table seems to have been used recently for the preparation of food. A passage leads to the west and a dark staircase can be seen leading upward. A dark chimney leads down and to the east is a small window which is slightly ajar.

rooms.name: Attic
rooms.description: This is the attic. The only exit is a stairway leading down.

rooms.name: Living Room
rooms.description: You are in the living room. To the east, a doorway opens into the kitchen. To the west is the front door, nailed shut. A trophy case stands against one wall, and a large oriental rug lies in the center of the room.

rooms.name: Cellar
rooms.description: You are in a dark and damp cellar with a narrow passageway leading north, and a crawlway to the south. On the west is the bottom of a steep metal ramp which is unclimbable.

rooms.name: The Troll Room
rooms.description: This is a small room with passages to the east and south and a forbidding hole leading west. Bloodstains and deep scratches (perhaps made by an axe) mar the walls.

rooms.name: East of Chasm
rooms.description: You are on the east edge of a chasm, the bottom of which cannot be seen. A narrow passage goes north, and the path you are on continues to the east.

rooms.name: Gallery
rooms.description: This is an art gallery. Most of the paintings have been stolen by vandals with exceptional taste. The vandals left through either the north or west exits.

rooms.name: Studio
rooms.description: This appears to have been an artist's studio. The walls and floors are splattered with paints of 69 different colors. Strangely enough, nothing of value is hanging here. At the south end of the room is an open door (also covered with paint). A dark and narrow chimney leads up from a fireplace; although you might be able to get up it, it seems unlikely you could get back down.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Dead End
rooms.description: You have come to a dead end in the maze.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike. A skeleton, probably the remains of a luckless adventurer, lies here.

rooms.name: Dead End
rooms.description: You have come to a dead end in the maze.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Dead End
rooms.description: You have come to a dead end in the maze.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Grating Room
rooms.description: You are in a small room near the maze. There are twisty passages in the immediate vicinity. Above you is a grating with a skull-and-crossbones lock.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Dead End
rooms.description: You have come to a dead end in the maze.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Maze
rooms.description: This is part of a maze of twisty little passages, all alike.

rooms.name: Cyclops Room
rooms.description: This room has an exit on the northwest, and a staircase leading up.

rooms.name: Strange Passage
rooms.description: This is a long passage. To the west is one entrance. On the east there is an old wooden door, with a large opening in it (about cyclops sized).

rooms.name: Treasure Room
rooms.description: This is a large room, whose east wall is solid granite. A number of discarded bags, which crumble at your touch, are scattered about on the floor. There is an exit down a staircase.

rooms.name: Reservoir South
rooms.description: There is a path along the stream to the east or west, a steep pathway climbing southwest along the edge of a chasm, and a path leading into a canyon to the southeast.

rooms.name: Reservoir
rooms.description: You are in a vast underground chamber, half-filled with cold dark water. The reservoir stretches north and south, fed from the north and drained by the dam to the south. Boats might cross when the water is high; at low tide, mud and silt stretch where the water once stood.

rooms.name: Reservoir North
rooms.description: There is a slimy stairway leaving the room to the north.

rooms.name: Stream View
rooms.description: You are standing on a path beside a gently flowing stream. The path follows the stream, which flows from west to east.

rooms.name: Stream
rooms.description: You are on the gently flowing stream. The upstream route is too narrow to navigate, and the downstream route is invisible due to twisting walls. There is a narrow beach to land on.

rooms.name: Mirror Room
rooms.description: You are in a large square room with tall ceilings. On the south wall is an enormous mirror which fills the entire wall. There are exits on the other three sides of the room.

rooms.name: Mirror Room
rooms.description: You are in a large square room with tall ceilings. On the south wall is an enormous mirror which fills the entire wall. There are exits on the other three sides of the room.

rooms.name: Cave
rooms.description: This is a tiny cave with entrances west and north, and a staircase leading down.

rooms.name: Cave
rooms.description: This is a tiny cave with entrances west and north, and a dark, forbidding staircase leading down.

rooms.name: Cold Passage
rooms.description: This is a cold and damp corridor where a long east-west passageway turns into a southward path.

rooms.name: Narrow Passage
rooms.description: This is a long and narrow corridor where a long north-south passageway briefly narrows even further.

rooms.name: Winding Passage
rooms.description: This is a winding passage. It seems that there are only exits on the east and north.

rooms.name: Twisting Passage
rooms.description: This is a winding passage. It seems that there are only exits on the east and north.

rooms.name: Atlantis Room
rooms.description: This is an ancient room, long under water. There is an exit to the south and a staircase leading up.

rooms.name: East-West Passage
rooms.description: This is a narrow east-west passageway. There is a narrow stairway leading down at the north end of the room.

rooms.name: Round Room
rooms.description: This is a circular stone room with passages in all directions. Several of them have unfortunately been blocked by cave-ins.

rooms.name: Deep Canyon
rooms.description: You are on the south edge of a deep canyon. Passages lead off to the east, northwest and southwest. A stairway leads down. You can hear a loud roaring sound, like that of rushing water, from below.

rooms.name: Damp Cave
rooms.description: This cave has exits to the west and east, and narrows to a crack toward the south. The earth is particularly damp here.

rooms.name: Loud Room
rooms.description: This is a large room with a ceiling which cannot be detected from the ground. There is a narrow passage from east to west and a stone stairway leading upward. The room is deafeningly loud with an undetermined rushing sound. The sound seems to be reverberating from all of the walls, making it difficult even to think.

variants.text: This is a large room with a ceiling which cannot be detected from the ground. There is a narrow passage from east to west and a stone stairway leading upward. The room is unnaturally quiet now, the rushing sound silenced as if it had never been.

rooms.name: North-South Passage
rooms.description: This is a high north-south passage, which forks to the northeast.

rooms.name: Chasm
rooms.description: A chasm runs southwest to northeast and the path follows it. You are on the south side of the chasm, where a crack opens into a passage.

rooms.name: Entrance to Hades
rooms.description: You are outside a large gateway, on which is inscribed|| Abandon every hope all ye who enter here!|| The gate is open; through it you can see a desolation, with a pile of mangled bodies in one corner. Thousands of voices, lamenting some hideous fate, can be heard.

rooms.name: Land of the Dead
rooms.description: You have entered the Land of the Living Dead. Thousands of lost souls can be heard weeping and moaning. In the corner are stacked the remains of dozens of previous adventurers less fortunate than yourself. A passage exits to the north.

rooms.name: Engravings Cave
rooms.description: You have entered a low cave with passages leading northwest and east.

rooms.name: Egyptian Room
rooms.description: This is a room which looks like an Egyptian tomb. There is an ascending staircase to the west.

rooms.name: Dome Room
rooms.description: You are at the periphery of a large dome, which forms the ceiling of another room below. Protecting you from a precipitous drop is a wooden railing which circles the dome.

rooms.name: Torch Room
rooms.description: This is a large room with a prominent doorway leading to a down staircase. Above you is a large dome. Up around the edge of the dome (20 feet up) is a wooden railing. In the center of the room sits a white marble pedestal.

rooms.name: Temple
rooms.description: This is the north end of a large temple. On the east wall is an ancient inscription, probably a prayer in a long-forgotten language. Below the prayer is a staircase leading down. The west wall is solid granite. The exit to the north end of the room is through huge marble pillars.

rooms.name: Altar
rooms.description: This is the south end of a large temple. In front of you is what appears to be an altar. In one corner is a small hole in the floor which leads into darkness. You probably could not get back up it.

rooms.name: Dam
rooms.description: You are standing on the top of the Flood Control Dam #3, which was quite a tourist attraction in times far distant. There are paths to the north, south, and west, and a scramble down.There is a control panel here, on which a large metal bolt is mounted. Directly above the bolt is a small green plastic bubble.

rooms.name: Dam Lobby
rooms.description: This room appears to have been the waiting room for groups touring the dam. There are open doorways here to the north and east marked "Private", and there is a path leading south over the top of the dam.

rooms.name: Maintenance Room
rooms.description: This is what appears to have been the maintenance room for Flood Control Dam #3. Apparently, this room has been ransacked recently, for most of the valuable equipment is gone. On the wall in front of you is a group of buttons colored blue, yellow, brown, and red. There are doorways to the west and south.

rooms.name: Dam Base
rooms.description: You are at the base of Flood Control Dam #3, which looms above you and to the north. The river Frigid is flowing by here. Along the river are the White Cliffs which seem to form giant walls stretching from north to south along the shores of the river as it winds its way downstream.

rooms.name: Frigid River
rooms.description: You are on the Frigid River in the vicinity of the Dam. The river flows quietly here. There is a landing on the west shore.

rooms.name: Frigid River
rooms.description: The river turns a corner here making it impossible to see the Dam. The White Cliffs loom on the east bank and large rocks prevent landing on the west.

rooms.name: Frigid River
rooms.description: The river descends here into a valley. There is a narrow beach on the west shore below the cliffs. In the distance a faint rumbling can be heard.

rooms.name: White Cliffs Beach
rooms.description: You are on a narrow strip of beach which runs along the base of the White Cliffs. There is a narrow path heading south along the Cliffs and a tight passage leading west into the cliffs themselves.

rooms.name: White Cliffs Beach
rooms.description: You are on a rocky, narrow strip of beach beside the Cliffs. A narrow path leads north along the shore.

rooms.name: Frigid River
rooms.description: The river is running faster here and the sound ahead appears to be that of rushing water. On the east shore is a sandy beach. A small area of beach can also be seen below the cliffs on the west shore.

rooms.name: Frigid River
rooms.description: The sound of rushing water is nearly unbearable here. On the east shore is a large landing area.

rooms.name: Shore
rooms.description: You are on the east shore of the river. The water here seems somewhat treacherous. A path travels from north to south here, the south end quickly turning around a sharp corner.

rooms.name: Sandy Beach
rooms.description:
You are on a large sandy beach on the east shore of the river, which is flowing quickly by. A path runs beside the river to the south here, and a passage is partially buried in sand to the northeast.

[DEV NOTE: dig-with-shovel scarab puzzle is unwired. The scarab is currently visible without digging — canonical Zork required four DIG WITH SHOVEL actions to unbury it.]

rooms.name: Sandy Cave
rooms.description:
This is a sand-filled cave whose exit is to the southwest.

[DEV NOTE: dig-with-shovel scarab puzzle is unwired. The scarab is currently visible here without digging — canonical Zork required four DIG WITH SHOVEL actions to unbury it.]

rooms.name: Aragain Falls
rooms.description: You are at the top of Aragain Falls, an enormous waterfall with a drop of about 450 feet. The only path here is on the north end.

variants.text: You are at the top of Aragain Falls, an enormous waterfall with a drop of about 450 feet. The only path here is on the north end. A solid rainbow spans the falls.

rooms.name: On the Rainbow
rooms.description: You are on top of a rainbow (I bet you never thought you would walk on a rainbow), with a magnificent view of the Falls. The rainbow travels east-west here.

rooms.name: End of Rainbow
rooms.description: You are on a small, rocky beach on the continuation of the Frigid River past the Falls. The beach is narrow due to the presence of the White Cliffs. The river canyon opens here and sunlight shines in from above. A rainbow crosses over the falls to the east and a narrow path continues to the southwest.

rooms.name: Canyon Bottom
rooms.description: You are beneath the walls of the river canyon which may be climbable here. The lesser part of the runoff of Aragain Falls flows by below. To the north is a narrow path.

rooms.name: Rocky Ledge
rooms.description: You are on a ledge about halfway up the wall of the river canyon. You can see from here that the main flow from Aragain Falls twists along a passage which it is impossible for you to enter. Below you is the canyon bottom. Above you is more cliff, which appears climbable.

rooms.name: Canyon View
rooms.description: You are at the top of the Great Canyon on its west wall. From here there is a marvelous view of the canyon and parts of the Frigid River upstream. Across the canyon, the walls of the White Cliffs join the mighty ramparts of the Flathead Mountains to the east. Following the Canyon upstream to the north, Aragain Falls may be seen, complete with rainbow. The mighty Frigid River flows out from a great dark cavern. To the west and south can be seen an immense forest, stretching for miles around. A path leads northwest. It is possible to climb down into the canyon from here.

rooms.name: Mine Entrance
rooms.description: You are standing at the entrance of what might have been a coal mine. The shaft enters the west wall, and there is another exit on the south end of the room.

rooms.name: Squeaky Room
rooms.description: You are in a small room. Strange squeaky sounds may be heard coming from the passage at the north end. You may also escape to the east.

rooms.name: Bat Room
rooms.description: You are in a small room which has doors only to the east and south. In the corner of the room on the ceiling is a large vampire bat who is obviously deranged and holding his nose.

rooms.name: Shaft Room
rooms.description: This is a large room, in the middle of which is a small shaft descending through the floor into darkness below. To the west and the north are exits from this room. Constructed over the top of the shaft is a metal framework to which a heavy iron chain is attached.

rooms.name: Smelly Room
rooms.description: This is a small nondescript room. However, from the direction of a small descending staircase a foul odor can be detected. To the south is a narrow tunnel.

rooms.name: Gas Room
rooms.description: This is a small room which smells strongly of coal gas. There is a short climb up some stairs and a narrow tunnel leading east.

rooms.name: Ladder Top
rooms.description: This is a very small room. In the corner is a rickety wooden ladder, leading downward. It might be safe to descend. There is also a staircase leading upward.

rooms.name: Ladder Bottom
rooms.description: This is a rather wide room. On one side is the bottom of a narrow wooden ladder. To the west and the south are passages leaving the room.

rooms.name: Dead End
rooms.description: You have come to a dead end in the mine.

rooms.name: Timber Room
rooms.description: This is a long and narrow passage, which is cluttered with broken timbers. A wide passage comes from the east and turns at the west end of the room into a very narrow passageway. From the west comes a strong draft.

rooms.name: Drafty Room
rooms.description: This is a small drafty room in which is the bottom of a long shaft. To the south is a passageway and to the east a very narrow passage. In the shaft can be seen a heavy iron chain.

rooms.name: Machine Room
rooms.description: This is a large, cold room whose sole exit is to the north. In one corner there is a machine which is reminiscent of a clothes dryer. On its face is a switch which is labelled "START". The switch does not appear to be manipulable by any human hand (unless the fingers are about 1/16 by 1/4 inch). On the front of the machine is a large lid.

variants.text: This is a large, cold room whose sole exit is to the north. In one corner there is a machine which is reminiscent of a clothes dryer. On its face is a switch which is labelled "START". The switch does not appear to be manipulable by any human hand (unless the fingers are about 1/16 by 1/4 inch). On the front of the machine is a large lid, which is open.

This is a large, cold room whose sole exit is to the north. In one corner there is a machine which is reminiscent of a clothes dryer. On its face is a switch which is labelled "START". The switch does not appear to be manipulable by any human hand (unless the fingers are about 1/16 by 1/4 inch). On the front of the machine is a large lid, which is closed.

rooms.name: Coal Mine
rooms.description: This is a nondescript part of a coal mine.

rooms.name: Coal Mine
rooms.description: This is a nondescript part of a coal mine.

rooms.name: Coal Mine
rooms.description: This is a nondescript part of a coal mine.

rooms.name: Coal Mine
rooms.description: This is a nondescript part of a coal mine.

rooms.name: Slide Room
rooms.description: This is a small chamber, which appears to have been part of a coal mine. On the south wall of the chamber the letters "Granite Wall" are etched in the rock. To the east is a long passage, and there is a steep metal slide twisting downward. To the north is a small opening.

items.name: board
items.description: The boards are securely fastened.

items.name: songbird
items.description: A small bright-feathered songbird, watching you with curious black eyes. Its trill is impossibly pure.
items.personality: A bright forest songbird with a clear, perfect trill. Doesn't speak — communicates through song. Curious about shiny things, especially the jewel-encrusted egg. Gentle, never threatening.

items.name: white house
items.description: The house is a beautiful colonial house which is painted white. It is clear that the owners must have been extremely wealthy.

items.name: forest
items.description: Dense pine and hemlock, the canopy filtering daylight to a green twilight.

items.name: tree
items.description: A large leaf-bearing tree, lower branches at climbing height.

items.name: mountain range
items.description: An impassable wall of high peaks — the Flatheads, named (legend has it) for their first king.

items.name: water
items.description: Cold dark water — drinkable, you suppose, though the thought isn't appetizing.

items.name: quantity of water
items.description: A measure of clear water, transparent and quite ordinary.

items.name: number of ghosts
items.description: Translucent figures drifting through the air, faces blank with ancient grief. They take no notice of you.

items.name: crystal skull
items.description: A beautifully carved crystal skull, grinning at you rather nastily. A treasure.

items.name: lunch
items.description: A hot pepper sandwich wrapped tightly in waxed paper. Smells fierce.

items.name: bat
items.description: A sleek vampire bat, watching with bright unblinking eyes — and a mouth full of small sharp teeth.

items.name: brass bell
items.description: A small brass bell with a worn handle, polished smooth by use.

items.name: bloody axe
items.description: A heavy iron axe, its head crusted with dried blood.

items.name: bolt
items.description: A heavy iron bolt with a slot evidently sized for a wrench.

items.name: green bubble
items.description: A small green glass bubble — currently dark, evidently waiting for something.

items.name: altar
items.description: A massive stone altar, carved with worn ritual figures and stained dark by centuries of offerings.
items.narratorNote: The altar is a flat ritual surface, not a closeable container. Items located 'in' the altar (candles, the black book) are actually resting ON top of it. Never refer to the altar as 'open' or 'closed'.

items.name: black book
items.description: A large black book bound in cracked leather, held open to page 569.

readable.text: Commandment #12592| | Oh ye who go about saying unto each: "Hello sailor":| Dost thou know the magnitude of thy sin before the gods?| Yea, verily, thou shalt be ground between two stones.| Shall the angry gods cast thy body into the whirlpool?| Surely, thy eye shall be put out with a sharp stick!| Even unto the ends of the earth shalt thou wander and| Unto the land of the dead shalt thou be sent at last.| Surely thou shalt repent of thy cunning.

items.name: sceptre
items.description: A sceptre, possibly that of ancient Egypt itself, is in the coffin. The sceptre is ornamented with colored enamel, and tapers to a sharp point.

items.name: broken timber
items.description: A length of broken timber, splintered at one end and surprisingly heavy.

items.name: chute
items.description: A long metal chute angling steeply downward into darkness. Once you commit, climbing back out looks impossible.

items.name: kitchen table
items.description: A long wooden kitchen table, scarred by years of food preparation.
items.narratorNote: This is a flat work surface, not a closeable container. Items located 'in' the kitchen-table (sandwich-bag, bottle) are actually resting ON its surface — narrate them as such. Never refer to the table as 'open' or 'closed'.

items.name: table
items.description: A heavy old wooden table, varnish worn through in patches.
items.narratorNote: Flat surface, not a closeable container. Items located 'in' this table are resting ON its surface. Never refer to the table as 'open' or 'closed'.

items.name: brown sack
items.description: An elongated brown paper sack, smelling powerfully of hot peppers.

items.name: group of tool chests
items.description: The chests are all empty.

container.accessBlockedMessage: The group of tool chests is closed.

items.name: yellow button
items.description: A worn yellow button, slightly recessed. Yellow paint flakes around the edges.

items.name: brown button
items.description: A small brown button, smooth and unmarked.

items.name: red button
items.description: A red button marked with a faded warning symbol.

items.name: blue button
items.description: A small blue button, smooth and unmarked.

items.name: trophy case
items.description: A handsome glass-fronted display case — clearly meant to hold a collector's prizes.

items.name: carpet
items.description: A heavy oriental carpet covers the center of the floor, oddly out of place. Something about its position invites curiosity.

items.name: chalice
items.description: A silver chalice, intricately engraved with looping vines. A treasure.

items.name: clove of garlic
items.description: A pungent clove of garlic, its papery skin crackling at the slightest touch.

items.name: crystal trident
items.description: Poseidon's own trident, carved from a single piece of crystal. A treasure.

items.name: cyclops
items.description: A cyclops, who looks prepared to eat horses (much less mere adventurers), blocks the staircase. From his state of health, and the bloodstains on the walls, you gather that he is not very friendly, though he likes people.
items.personality: A massive, single-eyed brute, hungry and angry. Speaks in slow rumbling Common — full sentences but simple words. Demands food and drink. Terrified of the name of the Greek hero who blinded his ancestor. If fed, becomes drowsy and friendly; if threatened, roars and lunges.

items.name: dam
items.description: Flood Control Dam #3 — a vast concrete structure of evident bureaucratic origin, holding back an enormous body of water.

items.name: boarded window
items.description: A window securely boarded over from within. There's no entering this way.

items.name: stone door
items.description: An enormous stone door, impossibly heavy. It would take more than human strength to budge.

items.name: stone barrow
items.description: A great mound of weathered stone, ancient beyond reckoning, with a single massive door.

items.name: glass bottle
items.description: A clear glass bottle, sealed at the neck.

items.name: crack
items.description: A narrow vertical crack — far too thin for anyone to slip through.

items.name: gold coffin
items.description: The solid-gold coffin used for the burial of Ramses II — heavy, ornate, and worth a king's ransom. A treasure.

items.name: hand-held air pump
items.description: A small hand-held pump — the sort you'd use for a bicycle tire, or perhaps a folded raft.

items.name: jade figurine
items.description: An exquisite jade figurine, carved with delicate features. A treasure.

items.name: nasty knife
items.description: A nasty-looking knife, the blade dull but evidently well-used.

items.name: skeleton
items.description: A weathered skeleton, long picked clean. A small leather bag lies near one outstretched hand.

items.name: burned-out lantern
items.description: A battered brass lantern, its battery long since drained dry.

items.name: leather bag of coins
items.description: An old leather bag, bulging with coins. A treasure.

items.name: brass lantern
items.description: A battery-powered brass lantern is on the trophy case.

items.name: large emerald
items.description: A flawless emerald the size of a small egg, its green depths catching every gleam of light. A treasure.

items.name: leaflet
items.description: A small printed leaflet, the paper a little crumpled.

readable.text: "WELCOME TO ZORK!| | ZORK is a game of adventure, danger, and low cunning. In it you will explore some of the most amazing territory ever seen by mortals. No computer should be without one!"

items.name: leak
items.description: Water trickles from a small leak — for now a nuisance rather than a disaster.

items.name: machine
items.description: A complicated piece of machinery: gears, curious nozzles, a hinged lid, and a small switch on one flank.

items.name: small mailbox
items.description: A small mailbox of dented metal, fixed firmly to a post outside the front door.

items.name: matchbook
items.description: A matchbook whose cover advertises FCD#3 in chipper bureaucratic prose.

readable.text: | (Close cover before striking)| | YOU too can make BIG MONEY in the exciting field of PAPER SHUFFLING!| | Mr. Anderson of Muddle, Mass. says: "Before I took this course I was a lowly bit twiddler. Now with what I learned at GUE Tech I feel really important and can obfuscate and confuse with the best."| | Dr. Blank had this to say: "Ten short days ago all I could look forward to was a dead-end job as a doctor. Now I have a promising future and make really big Zorkmids."| | GUE Tech can't promise these fantastic results to everyone. But when you earn your degree from GUE Tech, your future will be brighter.

items.name: mirror
items.description: An enormous wall-sized mirror, polished to a perfect reflection.

variants.text: The mirror is broken into many pieces.

items.name: mirror
items.description: An enormous wall-sized mirror, polished to a perfect reflection.

variants.text: The mirror is broken into many pieces.

items.name: painting
items.description: Fortunately, there is still one chance for you to be a vandal, for on the far wall is a painting of unparalleled beauty.

items.name: pair of candles
items.description: A pair of long burning candles, dripping wax steadily.

items.name: pile of bodies
items.description: A heap of corpses, far too long-dead to identify. Disturbing them feels like a very poor idea.

items.name: pile of leaves
items.description: A pile of dry leaves, gathered as if by wind.

items.name: pile of plastic
items.description: A folded square of yellow plastic with a small valve attached — clearly intended to be inflated.

vehicle.enterBlockedMessage: The boat is a flat pile of plastic — it'd need to be inflated first.

items.name: platinum bar
items.description: A heavy platinum bar, dense and gleaming dully. A treasure.

items.name: pot of gold
items.description: A heavy iron pot brimming with gold coins. A treasure.

items.name: prayer
items.description: An inscription in an ancient script — a prayer of safe passage, you somehow understand.

readable.text: The prayer is inscribed in an ancient script, rarely used today. It seems to be a philippic against small insects, absent-mindedness, and the picking up and dropping of small objects. The final verse consigns trespassers to the land of the dead. All evidence indicates that the beliefs of the ancient Zorkers were obscure.

items.name: wooden railing
items.description: A worn wooden railing, polished smooth by countless hands.

items.name: rainbow
items.description: A perfect arc of color, shimmering with a strange and almost solid presence.

items.name: river
items.description: Cold dark water moving with quiet, steady force. The current is swift; rocks lie half-hidden below.

items.name: red buoy
items.description: A red metal buoy, weathered by long exposure — evidently a warning.

items.name: rope
items.description: A long coil of strong rope.

items.name: rusty knife
items.description: A rusty knife, the blade pitted but still serviceable.

items.name: sand
items.description: Fine pale sand, soft underfoot — the sort that responds well to a shovel.

items.name: sapphire-encrusted bracelet
items.description: A delicate bracelet set with sapphires, glittering even in dim light. A treasure.

items.name: screwdriver
items.description: A standard flat-head screwdriver, well-worn but serviceable.

items.name: skeleton key
items.description: A skeleton key of dark iron, deeply worn — clearly very old.

items.name: shovel
items.description: A long-handled shovel, well-balanced for digging.

items.name: small pile of coal
items.description: A small heap of dusty black coal — surprisingly heavy, and very dirty.

items.name: wooden ladder
items.description: A sturdy wooden ladder, rungs worn smooth by use.

items.name: beautiful jeweled scarab
items.description: An exquisite scarab beetle carved from a single piece of jade and chased with gold. A treasure.

items.name: large bag
items.description: The bag is underneath the thief, so one can't say what, if anything, is inside.

items.name: stiletto
items.description: A slim, wickedly pointed stiletto — the thief's signature weapon.

items.name: switch
items.description: A small switch with a slot evidently sized for a screwdriver.

items.name: sword
items.description: An elvish sword of great antiquity, the blade etched with fine runes.

variants.text: An elvish sword of great antiquity, the blade etched with fine runes — and presently glowing with a faint blue light.

items.name: ancient map
items.description: In the trophy case is an ancient parchment which appears to be a map.

readable.text: The map shows a forest with three clearings. The largest clearing contains a house. Three paths leave the large clearing. One of these paths, leading southwest, is marked "To Stone Barrow".

items.name: thief
items.description: A suspicious-looking gentleman in a black cloak, holding a large bag and armed with a deadly stiletto.
items.personality: A suave, mustachioed gentleman thief in a black cloak. Speaks in clipped, ironic phrases ('How droll.', 'A trifle, surely?'). Will steal valuables on sight; pockets things and vanishes. Treats every encounter as an aesthetic event — never crude, always theatrical.

items.name: pedestal
items.description: A polished stone pedestal, waist-high.
items.narratorNote: A short column, not a container. Items located 'in' the pedestal (the torch) are resting ON its top. Never refer to the pedestal as 'open' or 'closed'.

items.name: torch
items.description: A flaming torch, the haft carved from ivory and the head wreathed in clean flame. A treasure.

items.name: tour guidebook
items.description: Some guidebooks entitled "Flood Control Dam #3" are on the reception desk.

readable.text: "	Flood Control Dam #3| | FCD#3 was constructed in year 783 of the Great Underground Empire to harness the mighty Frigid River. This work was supported by a grant of 37 million zorkmids from your omnipotent local tyrant Lord Dimwit Flathead the Excessive. This impressive structure is composed of 370,000 cubic feet of concrete, is 256 feet tall at the center, and 193 feet wide at the top. The lake created behind the dam has a volume of 1.7 billion cubic feet, an area of 12 million square feet, and a shore line of 36 thousand feet.| | The construction of FCD#3 took 112 days from ground breaking to the dedication. It required a work force of 384 slaves, 34 slave drivers, 12 engineers, 2 turtle doves, and a partridge in a pear tree. The work was managed by a command team composed of 2345 bureaucrats, 2347 secretaries (at least two of whom could type), 12,256 paper shufflers, 52,469 rubber stampers, 245,193 red tape processors, and nearly one million dead trees.| | We will now point out some of the more interesting features of FCD#3 as we conduct you on a guided tour of the facilities:| | 1) You start your tour here in the Dam Lobby. You will notice on your right that....

items.name: troll
items.description: A nasty-looking troll, brandishing a bloody axe, blocks all passages out of the room.
items.personality: A nasty, hulking creature with greasy black hair and broken yellow tusks. Speaks in broken Common — short sentences, aggressive, third person ('Troll smash!'). Hates elves and dwarves; covets shiny things. If addressed in a friendly tone, suspicious. If threatened, roars. If knocked unconscious, snores and mutters about gold. Will not converse meaningfully — short menacing lines only.

items.name: trunk of jewels
items.description: An old wooden trunk, mud-caked and bulging with jewels. A treasure.

items.name: tube
items.description: A small metal tube of the sort that holds toothpaste — but its contents are something else entirely.

readable.text: ---> Frobozz Magic Gunk Company <---| All-Purpose Gunk

items.name: viscous material
items.description: A lump of viscous greyish material, evidently good for sealing a puncture or two.

items.name: wall with engravings
items.description: Old engravings worked deep into stone — ancient writing, half worn away.

readable.text: The engravings were incised in the living rock of the cave wall by an unknown hand. They depict, in symbolic form, the beliefs of the ancient Zorkers. Skillfully interwoven with the bas reliefs are excerpts illustrating the major religious tenets of that time. Unfortunately, a later age seems to have considered them blasphemous and just as skillfully excised them.

items.name: ZORK owner's manual
items.description: Loosely attached to a wall is a small piece of paper.

readable.text: Congratulations!| | You are the privileged owner of ZORK I: The Great Underground Empire, a self-contained and self-maintaining universe. If used and maintained in accordance with normal operating practices for small universes, ZORK will provide many months of trouble-free operation.

items.name: cliff
items.description: A rough cliff face, weathered into ledges and handholds. Climbing it is possible — and perilous.

items.name: white cliffs
items.description: Sheer chalk-white cliffs, scoured smooth and quite unscalable.

items.name: wrench
items.description: A solid iron wrench, sized for a familiar bolt.

items.name: control panel
items.description: A bank of indicator lights, four colored buttons, and a single iron bolt — clearly some kind of maintenance station.

items.name: bird's nest
items.description: A small bird's nest, woven of twigs and grass.

items.name: jewel-encrusted egg
items.description: In the bird's nest is a large egg encrusted with precious jewels, apparently scavenged by a childless songbird. The egg is covered with fine gold inlay, and ornamented in lapis lazuli and mother-of-pearl. Unlike most eggs, this one is hinged and closed with a delicate looking clasp. The egg appears extremely fragile.
items.narratorNote: The egg, once opened, cannot be closed again. The clasp is delicate filigree; once sprung, it bends out of true and refuses to seat. If the player tries to close it, narrate the failure in flavor — the clasp is a one-shot mechanism, the gold inlay has buckled, the tiny hinge-pin was lost into the songbird's beak (or thief's pocket), etc. Pick a silly-but-period-appropriate reason; never say 'the engine doesn't allow it.'

items.name: golden clockwork canary
items.description: A golden clockwork canary with ruby eyes and a silver beak. A crystal window below its left wing reveals intricate machinery; the mechanism appears to have wound down. A treasure.

items.name: stairs
items.description: A narrow flight of stone steps ascending into shadow.

items.name: large basket
items.description: A large wicker basket suspended from a chain that disappears upward into the darkness of the shaft.

variants.text: A large wicker basket sits at your feet, attached to a chain that runs up through the shaft above into darkness.

items.narratorNote: The basket is a freight elevator on a chain. It can only be moved by 'lower basket' or 'raise basket' (use the lower-basket / raise-basket intents). It cannot be picked up or carried; nor can it be opened or closed (it has no lid). Treat it as a fixed feature whose only state is up vs. down.

items.name: huge diamond
items.description: A magnificent flawless diamond, gleaming with inner fire.

items.name: beautiful brass bauble
items.description: A small, glittering brass bauble — surprisingly heavy for its size. A treasure.

passages.name: kitchen window
passages.description: A small kitchen window. It appears to be slightly ajar.

glimpse.prompt: Through the window, describe the kitchen briefly — emphasize warmth and signs of recent cooking. Keep it to a sentence.
Through the window, describe the area behind the house briefly — open ground sloping toward forest. Keep it to a sentence.

variants.text: The kitchen window is open wide enough to climb through.

passages.name: trap door
passages.description: A wooden trap door is set into the floor.

passages.name: grating
passages.description: A heavy iron grating set into the floor — or, depending on which side you stand on, into the ceiling. Beyond it lies a clearing of scattered leaves.

passages.name: front door
passages.description: Solid oak crossed with thick iron bands. There is no handle on this side and no obvious way to open it.
passages.narratorNote: This door is sealed from within — the player cannot open it from either side. It is the canonical 'barred front door'. When the player tries to open it or go through it, narrate the door's solid construction (heavy oak, iron bands, no handle, no keyhole on this side). Direct them to find another way in (the kitchen window) or out (likewise).

sides.name: front door
sides.description: The handsome front door of the white house — solid oak, iron-banded, evidently barred from within.

sides.name: wooden door
sides.description: A heavy oak door crossed with thick iron bands — the front door, barred shut from this side. No handle, no keyhole.

passages.traverseBlockedMessage: The door is barred shut from the inside — solid oak, iron-banded, no handle on this side. You'll need to find another way through.

passages.name: chimney
passages.description: A dark, narrow chimney rising from the studio fireplace up to the kitchen above.

sides.traverseBlockedMessage: Only Santa Claus climbs down chimneys.
You can't get up there with what you're carrying.

customTools.description: Player asks about their current score, rank, moves, or progress (e.g. 'score', 'how am I doing?', 'what's my score?'). Always available.

handler.successNarration: Your score is {flag.score} (total of {flag.max-score}), in {flag.global-turn-count} moves. This gives you the rank of {rank}.

customTools.description: Player opens, pulls open, lifts the lid of, unlatches, or otherwise opens an item or passage (door, window, lid, etc.). Pass the id from the current view's itemsHere or passagesHere. The engine's handler reports failure when the target isn't accessible, isn't openable, or is already open — narrate from those cues.

itemId.description: the id of the item or passage to open (from itemsHere or passagesHere in the current view)

customTools.description: Player closes, shuts, latches, lids, or otherwise closes an item or passage (door, window, lid, etc.).

itemId.description: the id of the item or passage to close (from itemsHere or passagesHere in the current view)

customTools.description: Player breaks, smashes, shatters, hurls, or otherwise destroys an item. Once broken, an item stays broken — opening or repairing it requires a separate puzzle.

preconditions.failedNarration: {arg.itemId.name} doesn't seem like something you could break.

customTools.description: Player ties, fastens, attaches, lashes, secures, or otherwise affixes the rope to the railing (or to the dome).
Player gives the jewel-encrusted egg to the songbird, offers it to the bird, hands it to her, presents it to the singer.
Player gives, offers, hands, or presents the jewel-encrusted egg to the thief.
Player moves, slides, lifts, pulls aside, kicks aside, rolls up, or otherwise displaces the rug (carpet) in the living room.
Player says, shouts, whispers, or invokes 'Ulysses', 'Odysseus', or otherwise utters the magic word that terrifies the cyclops.
Player gives, offers, or hands the cyclops the lunch (hot pepper sandwich) and the bottle of water — feeding and watering him.
Player prays, kneels, makes an offering, or performs a ritual at the altar.
Player turns on, lights, switches on, fires up, or activates the brass lantern.
Player turns off, douses, switches off, or extinguishes the brass lantern.
Player tries to free, yank, pull, dislodge, retrieve, or wrench loose their stuck weapon (sword, knife, or axe).
Player tries to talk to, address, ask, shout at, insult, threaten, bargain with, beg, taunt, or otherwise engage the troll conversationally.
Player tries to talk to, address, ask, taunt, insult, threaten, or otherwise engage the cyclops conversationally — but NOT saying 'Ulysses' or 'Odysseus' (those match cyclops-magic-word instead).
Player says, shouts, calls, invokes, or otherwise utters the word 'echo' in the loud room.
Player waves, raises, brandishes, points, or otherwise invokes the sceptre at the rainbow.
Player winds, twists, turns the key on, cranks, or activates the clockwork canary.
Player brushes, polishes, scrubs, or cleans their teeth using the rubbery putty/glue/gunk.
Player sets fire to, ignites, or burns the pile of leaves (with match, candles, torch, or any flame).
Player sets fire to, ignites, or burns the black book (the ritual book of arcane lore).
Player leaps, jumps, dives, or hurls themselves down/off the current location (a tree, a canyon edge, a cliff).
Player desecrates, mutilates, destroys, or otherwise interferes with the pile of corpses at the entrance to Hades.
Player throws, hurls, or chucks an object directly at themselves.
Player attacks, slashes, stabs, or otherwise turns a weapon on themselves.
Player digs in the sand on the beach (with the shovel or by hand).
Player mutilates, defaces, slashes, or otherwise destroys the painting.
Player breaks, snaps, smashes, or otherwise destroys the sceptre (especially while at the rainbow).
Player jumps into, swims in, dives into, or wades through the Frigid River — without using the boat.
Player rubs, touches, presses, leans against, steps into, or otherwise interacts physically with the mirror.
Player rings, tolls, peals, sounds, or otherwise activates the bell.
Player reads, recites, intones, or speaks aloud from the black book at the entrance to Hades.
Player turns, presses, activates, or otherwise operates the machine's switch (with the screwdriver as a tool).
Player lowers, drops, sends down, releases, pays out, or chains down the basket / dumbwaiter / chain into the shaft.
Player raises, lifts, hauls up, draws up, pulls up, or winches up the basket / dumbwaiter / chain from the shaft.
Player pushes, presses, depresses, or activates the yellow button.
Player pushes, presses, depresses, or activates the brown button.
Player turns, twists, rotates, or otherwise operates the bolt on the dam control panel using the wrench.
Player inflates, pumps up, fills, or blows up the boat (with the pump).
Player plugs, repairs, patches, or applies putty to the punctured boat.
Player launches, shoves off, casts off, releases, or pays out the boat into the river.

triggers.narration: (Solving the dam puzzle. +15 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(Completing the bell-book-candle ritual at Hades. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(Ringing the bell at this threshold. +10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The troll defeated. +10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The cyclops dealt with. +10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The rainbow solidifies — passable now. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(A new passage opens. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You found the egg. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(Discovering the living room. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(Reaching the troll's lair. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(Discovering the cyclops's chamber. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the crystal skull's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the sceptre's worth — +4 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the chalice's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the crystal trident's worth — +4 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the gold coffin's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the huge diamond's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the jade figurine's worth — +5 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the leather bag of coins's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the large emerald's worth — +5 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the painting's worth — +4 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the platinum bar's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the pot of gold's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the sapphire-encrusted bracelet's worth — +5 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the beautiful jeweled scarab's worth — +5 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the torch's worth — +14 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the trunk of jewels's worth — +15 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the jewel-encrusted egg's worth — +5 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the golden clockwork canary's worth — +6 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
The crystal skull joins the trophy case's collection. (+10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The crystal skull leaves the trophy case. -10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The sceptre joins the trophy case's collection. (+6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The sceptre leaves the trophy case. -6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The chalice joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The chalice leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The crystal trident joins the trophy case's collection. (+11 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The crystal trident leaves the trophy case. -11 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The gold coffin joins the trophy case's collection. (+15 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The gold coffin leaves the trophy case. -15 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The huge diamond joins the trophy case's collection. (+10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The huge diamond leaves the trophy case. -10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The jade figurine joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The jade figurine leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The leather bag of coins joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The leather bag of coins leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The large emerald joins the trophy case's collection. (+10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The large emerald leaves the trophy case. -10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The painting joins the trophy case's collection. (+6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The painting leaves the trophy case. -6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The platinum bar joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The platinum bar leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The pot of gold joins the trophy case's collection. (+10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The pot of gold leaves the trophy case. -10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The sapphire-encrusted bracelet joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The sapphire-encrusted bracelet leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The beautiful jeweled scarab joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The beautiful jeweled scarab leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The torch joins the trophy case's collection. (+6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The torch leaves the trophy case. -6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The trunk of jewels joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The trunk of jewels leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The jewel-encrusted egg joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The jewel-encrusted egg leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The golden clockwork canary joins the trophy case's collection. (+4 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The golden clockwork canary leaves the trophy case. -4 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
You tie the rope securely to the railing. It hangs down into the room below.
You hold out the egg. The songbird hops to it and sings a single perfect trill — and the delicate clasp springs open, revealing a golden clockwork canary nestled inside.
You drag the rug aside. Beneath it, set flush into the floor, is a closed wooden trap door.

The cyclops's eye widens in stark terror. He bellows, scrambles backward, and crashes east through the wall — leaving a cyclops-shaped hole that opens straight into the living room. The way up the staircase is now unguarded.
The cyclops devours the sandwich in two bites and drains the bottle. Within moments his single eye droops, and he slumps against the wall, snoring thunderously. The staircase is no longer guarded.
You kneel before the altar. A gentle warmth gathers around you, and the temple fades. When your senses clear, you are standing in a sunlit forest.
The troll lies helpless. Your weapon descends and ends him with a single, brutal blow.

then.text: The axe whirls end-over-end and buries itself in the troll's chest. He topples.
The axe slams into the troll's shoulder, drawing blood. He pulls it free and drops it.

else.text: The axe sails wide and clatters against the wall.

then.text: Your rusty knife scratches the troll.
You stab the troll deep — the knife's tip snaps off.

else.text: The troll sneers and bats your knife aside.

then.text: Your blade bites the troll for a light wound; greenish blood wells.
Your blade carves a serious gash across the troll's chest.
A killing blow! The troll roars and topples in a pool of greenish blood.
Your blade strikes the troll's temple — he crumples, unconscious.
Your blade sweeps the troll's leg — he goes down hard.
Your blade cracks the troll's axe-grip — the bloody axe spins to the floor.
Your weapon sticks fast in the troll's hide — you can't pull it free!
The troll's axe nicks your arm.
The troll's axe bites deep!
The troll's axe strikes a mortal blow!

else.text: The troll swings wildly and misses.

triggers.narration: The troll heaves himself back to his feet.

then.text: You wrench the weapon free.

else.text: You pull, but the weapon stays fast.

triggers.narration: Your sword finally works free with a wet jerk.
Your axe finally works free with a wet jerk.
The troll collapses in a pool of greenish blood. His bloody axe falls at his feet. The east and west passages out of the room are no longer guarded.

effects.message:
**** You have died ****

Your breath rattles, and you slump beside the pile of bones. You have joined its inhabitants.

**** You have died ****

Your wounds overcome you, and the world fades to black.

triggers.narration: From above, an unseen hand slams the trap door shut. You hear the click of a latch.
The brass lantern flickers on, casting a warm glow.
The lantern goes dark.
Your lamp dims, flickers, and goes dark.

effects.message: Oh, no! You have walked into the slavering fangs of a lurking grue!

triggers.narration: The troll bares his yellow tusks and growls.
Your prattling has worn through the last shred of the troll's patience. 'TROLL SMASH!' he bellows, and lunges at you.
The cyclops eyes you with growing impatience.
The cyclops sniffs the air greedily and takes a step toward you.
The cyclops's patience snaps. 'I HUNGER!' he bellows, and his great hand swings down at you!

then.text: The cyclops cuffs you with the back of his hand — your vision swims.
The cyclops's fist pounds you into the wall!
The cyclops grabs you in a crushing grip — you feel ribs crack!
The cyclops's massive fingers find your throat. Stars bloom behind your eyes.

else.text: The cyclops swings — too slow. You dance aside.

then.text: Your blade glances off the cyclops's hide — a shallow scratch. He laughs.
Your blade finds a gap in the cyclops's hide and bites deep!

else.text: You swing — the cyclops doesn't even flinch. Your blade rings off his skin.

triggers.narration: The cyclops's massive bulk shudders, then crashes to the floor with a sound like a falling tree. He does not move again. The staircase is no longer guarded.
Somewhere in the darkness, you hear the soft scuff of a boot on stone. You are no longer alone in the underground.

then.text: A black-gloved hand snakes toward your pocket — you slap it away.
'A trifle, surely?' the thief murmurs, and the egg vanishes from your possession.
'How droll.' The bag of coins slips from your grasp into the thief's cloak.
The chalice is plucked from in front of you with a flick of the thief's wrist.
The painting — gone. The thief inclines his head in mock apology.
The jade figurine disappears into the folds of the thief's cloak.
The scarab — gone in a heartbeat. The thief tips an imaginary hat.
'A pleasure.' The bracelet is plucked from your wrist (or the floor) into the thief's palm.
Your blade scores a shallow cut across the thief's arm. He hisses.
Your blade bites deep. The thief reels, blood staining his fine cloak.
A clean killing strike. The thief sinks to his knees — 'How... unfortunate' — and topples.

else.text: The thief sidesteps your blade with a graceful pivot.

triggers.narration: The thief's body crumples. His cloak falls open and a glittering hoard of treasures spills out across the floor — whatever he had stolen, scattered across the Treasure Room.
The thief draws his stiletto with a sigh of regret — 'A duel, then. How... regrettable.'

then.text: The thief lunges; you sidestep — barely. The stiletto's tip whispers past your ear.
The stiletto bites — a shallow cut along your forearm, but it bleeds freely.
The blade slips past your guard and drives deep — you stagger, gasping.

else.text: A flick of the wrist, faster than you can follow — the stiletto plunges hilt-deep.

effects.message:
**** You have died ****

The thief, a man of culture, regrets the unpleasantness — but a duel is a duel. He wipes the stiletto on his cloak and is gone.

triggers.narration: The thief looks you over with cool calculation, weighing what you carry. He does not attack — yet.
The thief takes the egg between two long fingers, examines it, and gives a small approving smile. With an audible click the clasp yields. He pockets the egg, canary and all. 'A pleasure, as always.'
A cool draft seems to flow up from the floor of the temple — a passage somewhere has opened.
As you take the coffin in hand, the draft from below ceases. The way down has sealed itself again.

Your voice goes out — and instead of the cacophony you've been suffering, perfect silence answers. The acoustics of the room have changed. A platinum bar gleams on the floor where the noise had hidden it.

You raise the sceptre and trace a slow figure in the air. The rainbow shudders and solidifies — its colors hardening into a luminous bridge of gold and prismatic light. At your feet, a heavy pot of gold appears, gleaming where the rainbow now touches earth.

An almost inaudible voice whispers in your ear: 'Look to your treasures for the final secret.' Across the room, the trophy case glitters; in the depths of the wood, an ancient map appears — and far above ground, the way to a stone barrow stands open.

effects.message:
**** You have completed the Great Underground Empire ****

A voice calls out from the depths of the barrow: 'Welcome, adventurer. Your final score: {flag.score} (out of {flag.max-score}), in {flag.global-turn-count} moves. This earns you the rank of {rank}. Your name shall be inscribed in the chronicles of those who have braved the Empire and prevailed.'

The stone slab settles back into place behind you. Your adventure is at an end.

triggers.narration: You touch the cool surface of the mirror — and feel a brief, dizzying lurch. The room around you seems unchanged, yet subtly different, as if you've stepped through a hidden door.
You touch the cool surface of the mirror — and feel a brief, dizzying lurch. The room around you seems unchanged, yet subtly different, as if you've stepped through a hidden door.

then.text: Fweep! The bat seizes you by the scruff of the neck and lifts you away through utter blackness. You are deposited, dazed, in a coal mine.
Fweep! The bat carries you through tunnels you cannot see, depositing you in another part of the mine.
Fweep! The bat hauls you off into the darkness and drops you somewhere deep underground.
Fweep! The bat seizes you and bears you off to a distant shaft.
Fweep! The bat carries you up a narrow shaft and drops you at the top.
Fweep! The bat plummets with you down a long, dark drop and releases you at the bottom.
Fweep! The bat takes you on a winding journey and drops you in a squeaky-floored cavern.

else.text: Fweep! The bat carries you back near where the mining began.

triggers.narration: The bell tolls once — a deep, hollow note that hangs in the air far longer than seems natural. The metal grows warm in your grasp.

You read the words from page 569 aloud — and they hang in the air like smoke. The shrieks of the unseen wraiths fall silent, the candles flicker as if in a sudden wind, and a passage gapes open in the wall ahead. The Land of the Dead lies beyond.

The machine comes to life with a dazzling display of colored lights and bizarre noises — clattering, whirring, sparks. After a few moments the racket subsides. The lid pops open. Where you placed the coal, a magnificent diamond now lies.
The machine clatters and whirs, but produces only a faint puff of greenish smoke. Whatever it's looking for, it isn't finding.
The chain rattles as the basket descends out of sight, settling at the bottom of the shaft.
The chain creaks as you haul; the basket rises smoothly out of the depths and settles at the top of the shaft.
Click. A green bubble on the panel flickers and glows steadily.
Click. The green bubble dims and goes dark.
The bolt turns slowly. Somewhere below, the sluice gates open and water pours through the dam with a sustained roar. The reservoir will take some time to drain.
The bolt turns the other way. Below, the sluice gates rumble shut, and water begins to back up behind the dam once again.
Far below, the reservoir empties at last. What was deep water is now mud and silt — passable, if unpleasant.
The boat hisses and swells into a sturdy yellow raft, ready for the river.
You smear the putty over the gash. With a final hiss the rip seals; the boat is patched, deflated, and ready to inflate again. The tube of putty is now empty.
You shove off. The current catches the boat and carries you out onto the Frigid River.
Something sharp slips — the boat hisses, sputters, and collapses to a useless pile of plastic. You're spat out onto the riverbank at the foot of the dam.
The current carries you downstream.
The current carries you downstream, around a bend.
The current carries you faster — the rumble ahead grows louder.
The current sweeps you forward; the roar of falling water fills your ears. This is your last chance to land — east to the sandy beach!

effects.message:
**** You have died ****

Unfortunately, the magic boat doesn't provide any protection from the rocks and boulders one meets at the bottom of waterfalls. Including this one. Aragain Falls roars its indifference.

**** You have died ****

Oh dear. You seem to have ignited the coal gas. It's great for blowing up dimwitted adventurers who wander into a coal mine with an open flame.

triggers.narration: The egg hits the wooden floor of the branches with a sharp crack. The shell splits apart, exposing the clockwork canary — but its delicate mechanism is now hopelessly mangled.

The canary chirps, slightly off-key, an aria from a forgotten opera. From out of the greenery flies a lovely songbird. It perches on a limb just over your head and opens its beak to sing. As it does so a beautiful brass bauble drops from its mouth, bounces off the top of your head, and lands glimmering in the grass. As the canary winds down, the songbird flies away.

then.text: There is an unpleasant grinding noise from inside the canary.
The canary chirps blithely, but the songbird does not return.

else.text: The canary chirps, but its song carries little weight here.

triggers.narration: (You note the brass bauble's worth — +1 point. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
The brass bauble joins the trophy case's collection. (+6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The brass bauble leaves the trophy case. -6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)

effects.message:
**** You have died ****

Well, you seem to have been brushing your teeth with some sort of glue. As a result, your mouth gets glued together (with your nose), and you die of respiratory failure.

**** You have died ****

The leaves burn, and so do you.

**** You have died ****

As the knife approaches its victim, your mind is submerged by an overmastering will. Slowly, your hand turns, until the rusty blade is an inch from your neck. The knife seems to sing as it savagely slits your throat.

**** You have died ****

It's a long way down. You hit hard.

**** You have died ****

It's a shame the leap you just attempted has done you in.

**** You have died ****

At the moment the flame touches the book, a guardian materializes from the shadows. With a single, terrible gesture he reduces you to a fine ash.

**** You have died ****

A guardian materializes from the gloom. He shakes his head sadly, then places your own head on a sharp pole at the entrance to the temple.

**** You have died ****

If you insist...Poof, you're dead!

**** You have died ****

The momentum of your throw carries you backward; you crack your skull on the ground. Quite an achievement.

triggers.narration: You dig deeper into the loose sand. The hole grows.

effects.message:
**** You have died ****

The walls of the hole collapse around you, smothering you in cold, wet sand.

triggers.narration: You shred the canvas into ribbons. Whatever it depicted, it depicts no longer — the painting is now worthless.

effects.message:
**** You have died ****

The sceptre snaps in your hands. With it, the rainbow that bridged the falls fades and dissolves; you hang for a moment in empty mist before plummeting to the rocks below.

**** You have died ****

The Frigid River lives up to its name. The currents catch you and drag you under within seconds; you drown before you can struggle to the surface.

sharedVariants.text: It is pitch black. You are likely to be eaten by a grue.
